title: ophrys fusca subsp. lucentina
intro: Ophrys fusca subsp. lucentina, also known as the Bee Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid found in the Mediterranean region, particularly in Italy and Sardinia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Ophrys fusca subsp. lucentina include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as a natural expectorant, making it a valuable herb for those suffering from respiratory ailments. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
